methods.description: Process for isolating, maintaining, and genetically characterizing strains of C. socialis from spores collected in surface sediments.

steps.description: Strains of C. socialis (APC1, APC2, and APC12) were isolated from the germination of spores collected in surface sediments at station LTER-MareChiara in the Gulf of Naples.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate strains from spores collected in sediments

steps.description: Strain isolation, maintenance, and genetic characterization are illustrated in Pelusi et al. (2019).
steps.searchable_summary: refer to Pelusi et al. (2019) for detailed methods
outputs: detailed methods for strain maintenance and characterization

methods.description: Preparation of culture media and monitoring of experimental conditions for C. socialis strains.

steps.description: Culture media were prepared with artificial seawater at salinity of 36 (Sea salts, Sigma-Aldrich; Pelusi et al. 2019); ventilated tissue culture flasks (Corning) were used.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare culture media with artificial seawater at salinity 36

steps.description: Experiments were carried out at controlled temperature (18 C AE 1 C) and irradiance (~90 μmol photonsÁm −2 Ás −1 daily average) obtained with a sinusoidal cool-white illumination system at 12 : 12 h light : dark photocycle.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct experiments at controlled temperature and irradiance

steps.description: Light and temperature were monitored with a HOBO Pendant Temperature/Light Data Logger (Onset Computer Corporation).
steps.searchable_summary: monitor light and temperature with data logger

methods.description: Procedure for measuring cell and spore concentration in culture samples.

steps.description: To measure cell and spore concentration, 3 mL of culture were fixed with neutral formaldehyde at final concentration of 1.6% and counted with a Sedgewick-Rafter chamber on a Zeiss Axiophot microscope (ZEISS) at 400× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: fix culture with formaldehyde and count cells/spores using microscope
